title: Comparative Static Structural Mapping of Target-Site Herbicide Resistance Across Weed Enzyme Families
description: A reproducible comparative static structural-bioinformatics resource for verified weed target-site resistance positions across PPO, ALS/AHAS, EPSPS, and ACCase, with HPPD retained as a target-site-negative contrast family. For each accepted mutation the resource maps the position onto a herbicide-relevant structure, defines a ligand-contact active-site core, and reports within-family distance-to-core percentile, Tien-normalized relative solvent accessibility, and alignment-based conservation, with a within-family permutation enrichment test (run with and without direct-core positions) and a structural typology of how positions relate to the pocket.

creators.name: Chahal, Gourav
creators.affiliation: Department of Crop, Soil and Environmental Sciences, Auburn University, Auburn, AL, USA
